---
title: "Reflection"
---

## {{< fa book-open >}} My Learning Journey

::: {.callout-note}
This module pushed me to develop both technical and analytical skills I had no prior experience with,
particularly in version control and web development.
:::

---

## {{< fa code >}} Skills Developed

Throughout this module I worked across a range of tools and technologies:

| Skill | Tool | Application |
|---|---|---|
| Version Control | Git & GitHub | Managed all project changes carefully |
| Web Publishing | Quarto & GitHub Pages | Built this portfolio |
| Styling | SCSS & Bootswatch | Customised site theme and layout |

---

## {{< fa list-check >}} Module Checklist

Here is a short checklist which shows how this website has met the main requirements of this assessment:

- [x] Set up a Git repository and commit regularly
- [x] Deploy a Quarto website to GitHub Pages
- [x] Create all required pages (e.g About, Projects, Reflection)
- [x] Add a CV as a linked PDF 
- [x] Create a projects listing with executable Python code
- [x] Customise the theme with SCSS
- [x] Implement a satisfactory number of markdown features
- [x] Implement Google Font 
- [x] Clean, professional folder repository
- [x] Add all required social links

---

## {{< fa brain >}} Critical Reflection 

### What I Found Challenging 

To be honest, initially the entire assignment felt challenging. I was not familiar with GitHub very much, and found it a little intimidating to be constantly pushing things! There were a number of times I had to delete and recreate my repository because I just couldn’t get something to work. However, I did eventually get used to it. Initially, I also struggled with remembering to render with Quarto. Aside from this, I struggled with a few individual markdown items such as Mermaid diagrams, which I was unable to implement in the end. 

### What I Would Do Differently 

If I was starting again, I would definitely have a more established plan and list of things I wanted to get done. Whilst the final assignment rubric helped a lot of formulating a plan, I should’ve had one set up myself beforehand as it would’ve prevented chaotic additions and then retractions to my portfolio.

### How These Skills Apply Beyond This Module 

The combination of Git, Python, and web publishing is directly transferable to data analyst and business intelligence roles. Being able to present analysis in a professional, deployed portfolio rather than a static document is a necessary skill nowadays. An underrated part of this assignment is that it has also given us a GitHub commit history, which can be checked by some employers (particularly if I was to go into data analytics). I generally also got very comfortable with VS Code, which is a very commonly used and important software to be able to use.

## {{< fa arrow-right >}} Future Goals 
 
 I plan to keep using this portfolio after graduating to differentiate myself in a tight job market. I hope to add to it as I plan my own projects outside of university.
